key_themes: Geopolitical tailwinds and global rearmament supercycle (NATO spending, Ukraine contracts, Middle East tensions)
Strong 2025 results with beats on sales/EPS/FCF and positive 2026 guidance (5-6% organic sales growth)
Record backlog, major new contracts (e.g., $3.7B Patriot to Ukraine, multiple U.S. military awards)
Commercial aerospace recovery (Pratt & Whitney engine certifications, aftermarket demand)
Broad analyst support (mostly Buy ratings, avg. PT ~$219 vs. current ~$196-197)
summary: Sentiment around RTX is solidly positive amid a defense spending boom driven by geopolitical tensions and NATO rearmament commitments. The company posted strong 2025 results with revenue/EPS beats, a massive ~$268B backlog, and 2026 guidance calling for continued sales, earnings, and free-cash-flow growth; Pratt & Whitney and Collins Aerospace are also seeing commercial tailwinds. While the stock (currently ~$196-197 after a modest pullback) has already rallied sharply and faces Q1 earnings on April 21, analysts remain largely bullish on its long-term positioning.[[1]](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/04/17/should-you-buy-rtx-stock-before-the-huge-investor/)[[2]](https://www.rtx.com/news/news-center/2026/01/27/rtx-reports-2025-results-and-announces-2026-outlook-)[[3]](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/finterra-2026-4-15-rtx-corporation-navigating-the-global-rearmament-super-cycle)
sources: 15+ recent news articles (RTX investor releases, Motley Fool, Yahoo Finance, TipRanks, company announcements), 20+ X posts (earnings anticipation, defense themes, technical/outlook discussions from April 2026)
